## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, so let's talk about Evergreen launches. And so here's your initial launch. Let's say it's your first ever launch and you have your list here. And maybe you've been building your list for a few weeks or a few months or a few years, who knows, but you come out with a new product. And here's your good old sideways sales letter. And so you mail your list into your sideways sales letter and you have your launch. Fantastic. That's your internal launch. If it's your JV launch, maybe you have some partners and they mail into a squeeze page. They end up on your list and they go into your sideways sales letter. It's all fantastic. So at that point, you have to decide or you can decide or you get to decide and you don't have to make this decision once. You can make this decision whenever you want. But then you could just take this product and remember when you have your open cart, there's some period where there is this launch offer, right? So you have this launch offer and people have to buy by the end of your launch to get your launch offer, which is going to be some fantastic amazing offer that gets people to buy. So at this point, you can either leave this product on the market forever. Again, there would be a somewhat diminished offer because they didn't buy during the launch. So you can leave that product on the market forever. That's just fine. Or you could do periodic launches. And many of my pilaf owners, what they do is once a year, so here's our calendar. This is a year long and once a year or twice a year, they'll do a launch. So maybe they'll do a launch here and they'll make a lot of money. Or they might do two launches a year. I did that for a long time with back in my old stock market business where I had newsletters and products about the stock market and I also did it with pilaf. So it's all fantastic. Then the innovation after several years of doing this is that we started doing Evergreen. And now with Evergreen, basically we're going to leave off this joint venture piece. But now whenever anyone joins your list, shortly after they join your list, then they go through their own private launch. So if Fred joins your list on June 1st, a few days later, he would go into your sideways sales letter and he would go through the launch. And if Susan joined your list in March, she would go through her own launch. And if Sam joined your list in September, he would go through his own launch. So the idea is you're always having people join your list and you want to get the benefits of the launch so they go through the sideways sales letter.

Now let me tell you about why evergreen is awesome. So my business used to look like this. I had two launches a year. Then actually I dropped it down to one launch a year and then that one launch a year would look like this. So I ended up doing about as much as I did in those two launches, I would do in the one launch. But then you have this weird revenue graph where you're making a huge portion of your revenue during that launch. And the thing is that sometimes things go wrong in launches. Now, it's actually more complicated than that because there are times of the year where we don't run our evergreen launches. Like we'll shut down our evergreen launch for the month before our big launch. But it smooths out your revenue. And would you rather have this graph or would you rather have something like this? It's just it reduces the risk in your business. It helps your planning. And it's just a better way to run your business to have consistent sales coming in. Now another key piece is it helps you in some markets, it helps you serve the market better. ## [06:04 - 09:05]

If you have this one spike in the middle right here and you're, if you want to buy traffic to go into your list to build your list throughout the year, if you're spending money here and here and here, you don't want to wait 10, 11 months to see if that traffic is going to pay off or you don't even want to invest that money and wait for 10 or 11 years for the payoff. Another benefit of evergreen is that it allows you to test things. So this is something I do is that we'll be in evergreen, you know, here's our big launch and then we'll be in evergreen and we'll be testing stuff. We'll be testing landing pages, we'll be testing offers, we'll be testing different things. And so by the time we get to here, when it's really showtime for our big JV launch, we'll have a better offer, we'll have a better launch, we'll have a better copy, everything will be better because we'll be testing throughout the year. ## [09:05 - 12:06]

How you're going to change their life by getting your product. And so you owe it to them to put together a really good sales process. ## [12:06 - 13:08]

Once you actually start to have multiple offers in your business and multiple products in your business, you can actually then take them and run them through the next launch and then through the next launch.
